h1: Shiba Inu Puppies for Sale in Singapore

entity_definitions.definition: Curious Tails is an AVS licensed pet shop at 2 Balestier Road (licence AS24J00046) selling Shiba Inu puppies at $2,888 to $4,488 all-in, with the housing-fit conversation first.

The Shiba Inu is a Japanese spitz breed: 8 to 10 kg fully grown, fox-faced with a curled tail, famously independent, clean and cat-like in habits, with a double coat that sheds heavily in seasonal blows. In Singapore, Shibas are NOT on the HDB-approved list.

faq.question: Can I keep a Shiba Inu in an HDB flat?
faq.answer_direction: No; Shibas are not on the HDB-approved list. Condo or landed: yes. HDB readers: see the alternatives with a similar independent spirit.

faq.question: How much does a Shiba Inu cost in Singapore?
faq.answer_direction: Market roughly $4,000 to $8,000; ours $2,888 to $4,488 all-in. A Shiba far below market is a verify-the-licence-first situation, not a deal.

faq.question: Can a Shiba be left alone during work hours?
faq.answer_direction: Better than most breeds; independence is the breed's signature. The routine and pre-work exercise still decide the outcome.

faq.question: Can a Shiba go off-leash?
faq.answer_direction: Treat recall as unreliable for life; leash or secured areas only, with a well-fitted harness. This is breed reality, not pessimism.

faq.question: How bad is Shiba shedding?
faq.answer_direction: The double coat blows heavily about twice a year; daily brushing in those weeks, never shave.

faq.question: Is a Shiba a good first dog?
faq.answer_direction: Honestly: usually not the easiest one. If you want the look with a softer learning curve, message us your routine and we will tell you straight.

faq.question: What happens after I reserve?
faq.answer_direction: Deposit, free boarding, scheduled delivery, setup + walkthrough, week-one training lesson.
